title: andryala glandulosa subsp. cheiranthifolia
intro: Andryala glandulosa subsp. cheiranthifolia, also known as the Cheiranthifolia subsp. of the Andryala glandulosa plant, is a subspecies of the Andryala glandulosa plant, a type of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Andryala glandulosa subsp. cheiranthifolia include its ability to reduce inflammation, treat wounds, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
